user_query:
Help me stand up a simple local job application tracker for the three applications in input/applications.jsonl. Read that file and add each application using the provided fields (map JSON keys like applied_on -> --applied-on, next_follow_up -> --next-follow-up, contact_name -> --contact-name, etc.). After adding, update the application for company "Contoso" and role "ML Engineer" to status "interview", add the note "Recruiter screen passed; schedule technical", and set the next follow-up to 2026-04-06.

Then:
- List all applications with status "interview" and save the exact JSON output to output/list_interview.json.
- Show follow-ups due starting 2026-04-01 over a 10-day window and save that exact JSON to output/due_apr1_10days.json.
- Generate a pipeline summary and save the exact JSON to output/summary.json.

For reproducibility, also save:
- The raw JSON outputs from each add operation (one JSON object per line) to output/add_responses.jsonl.
- The raw JSON output from the update operation to output/update_response.json.

Finally, write a short human-readable pipeline summary to output/pipeline.md that includes:
- Bullet points with "By status" counts matching the JSON summary
- A "Next actions" section referencing at least one date within 2026-04-01 to 2026-04-11
- A line that mentions "Contoso — ML Engineer: interview"

Use only relative paths (input/, output/). Do not invent missing data; if a required field is absent for any record, call it out and skip that record instead of adding bad data.
